hero.one_line_summary: Globalization is the process by which countries and societies become more closely connected through trade, technology, communication, culture, and migration.
hero.memorable_example: Advances in transportation and communication have accelerated globalization.

meanings.definition: the process through which countries, economies, cultures, and people become increasingly connected and dependent on one another around the world
examples: Globalization has allowed companies to sell their products in markets around the world.
Advances in transportation and communication have accelerated globalization.
Critics argue that globalization can weaken local industries and traditions.
The course examines how globalization has influenced employment and migration.
Globalization has made it easier for ideas and cultural products to cross national borders.
Some developing countries have benefited from globalization through increased trade and investment.
The environmental effects of globalization extend far beyond individual national borders.
meanings.typical_pattern: globalization of + industry, trade, culture, or economy
meanings.register: neutral to formal; very common in economics, politics, sociology, business, history, and environmental studies

nuance.central_nuance: The word describes a broad process involving the movement of goods, money, information, technology, people, and cultural influences across national borders.
nuance.usual_implication: Globalization may create opportunities and greater cooperation, but it may also increase inequality, economic dependence, cultural pressure, or environmental harm.
nuance.contrast_explanation: Internationalization often describes a deliberate effort by a company, university, or organization to operate in multiple countries. Globalization is the broader process through which the world as a whole becomes increasingly interconnected.

grammar.how_to_use: Use globalization as the name of a general process.
grammar.what_can_follow: It is often followed by of and a field or activity, as in the globalization of trade. It can also be followed by verbs such as increase, accelerate, transform, affect, or contribute.
grammar.preposition_note: Use the globalization of something when identifying the area becoming more global, as in the globalization of higher education.
grammar.noun_use: Globalization is normally uncountable, so do not use a or an before it. The form globalizations is extremely rare and is not normally needed by learners.
grammar.position_note: It can appear without the when discussing the process generally. Use the when referring to a specific form or aspect, as in the globalization of financial markets.

patterns.use: Use this to identify the industry, activity, institution, or cultural area becoming more internationally connected.
patterns.example: The globalization of food production has created complex international supply chains.

patterns.use: Use this to describe a result of growing international interconnectedness.
patterns.example: Globalization has led to greater competition between manufacturers.

patterns.pattern: globalization has made it easier to + verb
patterns.use: Use this to explain an activity that has become easier because national borders matter less.
patterns.example: Globalization has made it easier to exchange scientific knowledge.

patterns.pattern: the effects or impact of globalization
patterns.use: Use this when discussing the consequences of globalization.
patterns.example: The study investigates the impact of globalization on small farming communities.

patterns.use: Use this for a force that causes countries and societies to become connected more rapidly.
patterns.example: Digital technology has helped drive globalization.

errors.wrong: The globalization has changed modern business.
errors.explanation: Do not normally use the when discussing globalization as a general process.

errors.wrong: A globalization has created new economic opportunities.
errors.correct: Globalization has created new economic opportunities.
errors.explanation: Globalization is normally uncountable, so it is not used with a.

errors.wrong: Globalization between trade has increased rapidly.
errors.correct: The globalization of trade has increased rapidly.
errors.explanation: Use the globalization of something, not globalization between something.

errors.wrong: Globalization only means that companies sell products abroad.
errors.correct: Globalization includes international trade as well as growing cultural, technological, political, and social connections.
errors.explanation: The meaning is broader than business or trade alone.

word_family.definition: relating to the whole world or involving many parts of the world
examples: Climate change is a global problem that requires international cooperation.
The company has developed a global network of suppliers.
word_family.relationship_to_headword: This adjective describes something involving or affecting the world as a whole.
word_family.grammar_pattern: global + economy, market, problem, network, trade, or population

word_family.definition: throughout the world or in a way that involves the whole world
examples: The disease affects millions of people globally.
The organization operates both locally and globally.
word_family.relationship_to_headword: This adverb describes something happening or existing worldwide.
word_family.grammar_pattern: operate, compete, spread, or be recognized globally

word_family.definition: to make a business, industry, activity, or system operate or develop throughout the world
examples: The company plans to globalize its production network.
Digital platforms have helped globalize the entertainment industry.
word_family.relationship_to_headword: This verb describes causing something to become more global.
word_family.grammar_pattern: globalize + industry, market, business, production, or culture
word_family.usage_note: The verb is less common than globalization and is mainly used in formal economic and business contexts.

collocations.example: Economic globalization has increased trade and investment between countries.
collocations.meaning: the increasing international connection of economies, markets, and businesses

collocations.example: Cultural globalization has helped music and films reach international audiences.
collocations.meaning: the spread and exchange of cultural products, practices, and ideas across countries

collocations.example: The globalization of trade has created highly complex supply chains.
collocations.meaning: the process through which trade becomes increasingly international
collocations.grammar_pattern: the globalization of + trade, industry, or market

collocations.example: The process of globalization began long before the development of the internet.
collocations.meaning: the gradual development of stronger connections between countries

collocations.example: Local businesses have struggled to adapt to the forces of globalization.
collocations.meaning: the economic, technological, political, and cultural pressures that increase global connections

collocations.example: The article explores the impact of globalization on traditional communities.
collocations.meaning: the effects that globalization has on people, institutions, or societies
collocations.grammar_pattern: the impact of globalization on + group or area

collocations.example: The effects of globalization are not distributed equally among countries.
collocations.meaning: the positive or negative consequences of growing global connections
collocations.grammar_pattern: the effects of globalization on + noun

collocations.example: Supporters emphasize the benefits of globalization for trade and innovation.
collocations.meaning: the advantages created by stronger international connections

collocations.example: The documentary examines the social and environmental costs of globalization.
collocations.meaning: the negative consequences or disadvantages of globalization

collocations.example: Faster communication technologies have accelerated globalization.
collocations.meaning: cause globalization to develop more rapidly

collocations.example: International investment and digital communication continue to drive globalization.
collocations.meaning: act as an important cause of globalization

collocations.example: Globalization has led to greater cultural exchange between many societies.
collocations.meaning: globalization has produced a particular result
collocations.grammar_pattern: globalization has led to + noun or -ing form

synonyms.difference: Global integration emphasizes different economies, systems, or societies becoming connected parts of a larger whole. Globalization is broader and can also include the spread of culture, information, technology, and social practices.
synonyms.example: Regional trade agreements have encouraged global integration.
synonyms.shared_meaning: Both expressions describe increasing connections between countries and societies.

synonyms.difference: Internationalization often describes a deliberate effort by a company, university, or institution to expand its international activities. Globalization usually refers to the wider historical process affecting the world as a whole.
synonyms.example: The university's internationalization strategy includes recruiting more overseas students.
synonyms.shared_meaning: Both involve increasing activity and connections across national borders.

antonyms.opposition: Deglobalization is the process through which international economic, political, or social connections become weaker or less extensive.
antonyms.example: Trade restrictions may contribute to deglobalization.
antonyms.usage_note: The word is especially common in economics and political analysis.

confusables.key_difference: Globalization is the process through which the world becomes more interconnected. Globalism is a belief, policy, or way of thinking that supports organizing activities and cooperation at a global level.
confusables.headword_example: Globalization has increased the international movement of goods and information.
confusables.confusable_example: The politician criticized globalism and called for greater national independence.
confusables.why_confused: Both words relate to global connections, but one names a process while the other often names an idea or policy.

confusables.key_difference: Globalization is a broad worldwide process, while internationalization often refers to a specific organization deliberately expanding its international operations.
confusables.headword_example: Globalization has transformed patterns of international trade.
confusables.confusable_example: The internationalization of the university included creating exchange programs.
confusables.why_confused: The two processes both involve activity across national borders and may occur together.

etymology.origin: The word is formed from global and the noun-forming ending -ization, which expresses the process of making or becoming something.

word_family_choice.prompt_sentence: Climate change requires a coordinated _____ response.
word_family_choice.completed_sentence: Climate change requires a coordinated global response.
word_family_choice.explanation: Use the adjective global before the noun response.

word_family_choice.prompt_sentence: The company sells its products _____ through online platforms.
word_family_choice.completed_sentence: The company sells its products globally through online platforms.
word_family_choice.explanation: Use the adverb globally to describe where the company sells its products.

word_family_choice.prompt_sentence: Digital communication has contributed greatly to _____.
word_family_choice.completed_sentence: Digital communication has contributed greatly to globalization.
word_family_choice.explanation: Use the noun globalization after contributed to.

word_family_choice.prompt_sentence: The business hopes to _____ its services over the next decade.
word_family_choice.completed_sentence: The business hopes to globalize its services over the next decade.
word_family_choice.explanation: Use the verb globalize after hopes to.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: Globalization has increased communication between distant regions.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: Globalization is correctly used without an article to refer to the general process.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: The globalization has created both opportunities and risks.
correct_or_incorrect.corrected_sentence: Globalization has created both opportunities and risks.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: Do not normally use the when referring to globalization in general.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: The article discusses the globalization of higher education.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: The globalization of something is a standard pattern.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: A globalization has affected every national economy.
correct_or_incorrect.corrected_sentence: Globalization has affected every national economy.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: Globalization is normally uncountable and is not used with a.

correct_or_incorrect.sentence: Globalization and internationalization always mean exactly the same thing.
correct_or_incorrect.corrected_sentence: Globalization and internationalization are related but do not always mean exactly the same thing.
correct_or_incorrect.explanation: Globalization is a broad worldwide process, while internationalization often describes the deliberate international expansion of a particular organization.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: The report examines the impact of globalization _____ rural communities.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: The report examines the impact of globalization on rural communities.
collocation_practice.explanation: Use the impact of globalization on a group, place, or activity.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: New transportation systems helped _____ globalization during the twentieth century.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: New transportation systems helped accelerate globalization during the twentieth century.
collocation_practice.explanation: Accelerate globalization means cause the process to develop more rapidly.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: The globalization _____ trade has connected producers with distant markets.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: The globalization of trade has connected producers with distant markets.

collocation_practice.prompt_sentence: The course considers both the benefits and the _____ of globalization.
collocation_practice.completed_sentence: The course considers both the benefits and the costs of globalization.
collocation_practice.explanation: The costs of globalization refers to its negative consequences.
